Dev.releaseNotes:
`fix`: **Nothing to see here, move along**:

…totally didn't forget to remove them from the constructor in the last commit.
`fix`: **Revert faulty `Lazy<TService>` usage as ASP.NET did not like that**.

`refactor` (`tmdb`): **Replace Polly rate limiter with SlidingWindowRateLimiter** ([#1352](https://github.com/ShokoAnime/ShokoServer/pull/1352)): (_Skip CI_)

Replaced `AsyncRateLimitPolicy` with a purpose-built `TmdbRateLimiter`
backed by the BCL `SlidingWindowRateLimiter`, fixing thundering-herd
retries, silent exception swallowing, and unbounded 429 retries.

- Added `TmdbRateLimiter` (sealed, DI singleton, `IDisposable`) with
  server 429 backoff, per-caller jitter, safe shutdown via `_disposeCts`,
  and deferred disposal of the old limiter on settings save
- Added `TmdbRateLimitSettings` (defaults: 30 req/s window)
- Fixed `ProcessWithConcurrencyAsync`: `try/catch` around `await task`
  (not `WhenAny`), `List<Task>` → `HashSet<Task>`, off-by-one in concurrency
  guard, `OperationCanceledException` matched by token
- Capped remote 429 retries at 10; retained `GeneralHttpException` in the
  handle chain so `OnTmdbRetryAsync` still fires for logging
- Partial cast/crew failures log a warning instead of aborting the job
- `PurgeUnlinkedPeople` and `RepairMissingPeople` converted to concurrent
- Season fetching in `UpdateShowSeasonsAndEpisodes` runs concurrently up
  to `_maxConcurrency`; fixed pre-existing `episodesToAdd++` race
- `ImportJob` catches `AggregateException` from
  `RunImport_PurgeUnlinkedTmdbPeople` — partial purge must not abort

`refactor` (`relocation`): **Split relocation service and preset manager**:

Split `IVideoRelocationService` into two separate interfaces to separate
concerns. Created `IRelocationPresetManager` for preset CRUD operations,
settings (`RenameOnImport`, `MoveOnImport`,
`AllowRelocationInsideDestinationOnImport`), and preset lifecycle events
(`PresetStored`, `PresetUpdated`, `PresetDeleted`). Slimmed
`IVideoRelocationService` to provider registration, relocation execution,
and utility methods.

Updated `RelocationPresetInfo` to accept `RelocationProviderInfo?`
eagerly at construction instead of resolving it lazily via the service,
so it only depends on `IRelocationPresetManager` for `UpdatePreset`
calls. All construction sites in `VideoRelocationService` now resolve
`providerInfo` via `GetProviderInfo()` before creating the wrapper.

`VideoRelocationService` implements both interfaces as a single
singleton, registered via `VideoRelocationService` with two interface
mappings in DI.

`refactor` (`groups`): **Unify group management under new `IShokoGroupManager`**:

Introduced `IShokoGroupManager` in `Shoko.Abstractions.Metadata.Services`
as a full CRUD and management interface for Shoko groups, replacing inline
repo/service calls scattered across controllers, v1 code, and model
helpers. It follows the existing `<type>Manager` naming convention used by
existing managers such as `IFilterPresetManager`, `IRelocationPresetManager`,
and `IImageManager`.

The main interface covers: CRUD (`GetAllGroups`, `GetGroupByID`, `CreateGroup`,
`MoveSeries`, `SetMainSeries`, `UpdateGroup`, `DeleteGroup`); auto-grouping
settings (`IsAutoGroupingEnabled`, `UseAutoGroupingRelationWeighting`,
`AutoGroupingRelationExclusions`, `AllowDissimilarTitleExclusion`); management
(`RenameAllGroups`); and events (`GroupAdded`, `GroupUpdated`, `GroupRemoved`,
`SeriesMoved`, `GroupsRecreated`).

Added event infrastructure: `GroupInfoUpdatedEventArgs`,
`SeriesMovedEventArgs`, `ShokoEventHandler` integration, and
`GroupEventEmitter` for SignalR notifications. `AnimeGroupCreator` fires
group lifecycle events during auto-grouping.

Added typed input models `GroupData` and `GroupUpdateData` for
create/update operations. `GroupData` exposes `Series`, `Groups`,
`Name`, `Description`, `ParentGroup`, and `MainSeries`. `GroupUpdateData`
uses flagged properties (`HasName`, `HasDescription`, `HasMainSeries`,
`HasParentGroup`) to distinguish "not provided" from "explicitly set to
null/clear", alongside simple collection properties `Groups` and `Series`
for additive membership changes.

Moved validation from the API layer into the new manager. deleting a group now
correctly auto-creates individual groups for orphaned series when `deleteSeries`
is `false`.

Updated all consumers — `GroupController`, `SeriesController`,
`ActionController`, v1 implementation, `SettingsProvider`, and the
`Group.MergeWithExisting` helper — to use `IShokoGroupManager`.

Added guard clauses to `AnimeGroupRepository.GetByParentID` and
`AnimeSeriesRepository.GetByAnimeID`/`GetByGroupID` for zero/negative IDs.

`chore`: **Sort imports & remove unused imports**.

`chore` (`abstractions`): **Bump abstractions version to 6.0.0-alpha.48**.

`chore`: **Remove unnecessary casts**.

`refactor` (`filtering`): **Consolidate filter interfaces and move hierarchy logic to service**:

Renamed `IFilterPreset` to `IFilter` as the abstract filter interface
(no `IsDirectory`), and `IStoredFilterPreset` to `IFilterPreset` as the
persisted preset interface (gained `IsDirectory`). `GenericFilterPreset`
became `GenericFilter` implementing `IFilter`. The engine and services
now use `filter is IFilterPreset { IsDirectory: true }` instead of
`filter.IsDirectory` when checking if it is a directory.

Added `IsAllowedToSee(IShokoGroup)`, `IsAllowedToSee(IShokoSeries)`,
and `IsAllowedToSee(IAnidbAnime)` to the `IUser` interface, exposing the
tag-based permission checks in the abstraction layer. The filtering
engine and service now call `user.IsAllowedToSee()` instead of casting
to `JMMUser` and calling `AllowedGroup`/`AllowedSeries`, making them
completely user-agnostic.

Added three methods to `IMetadataFilteringService`:
`GetTopLevelFilteredGroups`, `GetFilteredSubGroups`, and
`GetFilteredSeriesInGroup` — consolidating the group chain building,
hierarchy scoping, and fallback sort logic that previously lived across
four private methods in `FilterController`. And introduced `FilteredGroupResult`
with `GroupIDChains` (plural, `IReadOnlyList<IReadOnlyList<int>>`) to carry all
hierarchy paths from top-level group to matching sub-groups, along with the
matching `SeriesIDs` set.

Renamed `ISortingCriteriaHelp` to `ISortingExpressionHelp`,
`GetAvailableSortingCriteria` to `GetAvailableSortingExpressions`, and
the static helpers `GetSortingCriteriaHelp` to `GetSortingExpressionHelp`
in `ExpressionDiscovery` and all call sites.

Added `AllParentGroups` to `IShokoGroup` with an explicit interface
implementation on `AnimeGroup` delegating to the existing
`AllGroupsAbove`.

`FilterController` lost ~150 lines of hierarchy/scoping logic to the
service layer. The redundant `AllowedGroup`/`AllowedSeries` checks were
not carried forward because `FilteringEngine` already applies them
during filter evaluation.

Updated `Group` DTO constructor to accept `IReadOnlySet<int>?` instead
of `HashSet<int>?` for `seriesIDs`.

`chore` (`abstractions`): **Bump abstractions version to 6.0.0-alpha.47**.
`fix`: **Fix EF Setup for Multiple Backends**.
`misc`: **Hopefully fix duplicate job key saving**.

`repo` (`workflows`): **Update deprecated GitHub Actions to current versions - Review - this is purely to bring all actions up to date - feedback requested** ([#1350](https://github.com/ShokoAnime/ShokoServer/pull/1350)):

Updated all four workflow files to remove deprecated or removed action
versions and fix one breaking input rename introduced by the v3 upgrade.

**Version bumps:**
- `actions/checkout`: `@master` → `@v4` (build-release, docker-manual, tray-standalone-manual)
- `actions/setup-dotnet`: `v3` → `v4` (all workflows)
- `actions/upload-artifact`: `v3` → `v4` (tray-standalone-manual; v3 was removed by GitHub Nov 2024)
- `actions/github-script`: `v6` → `v7` (build-release)
- `docker/setup-qemu-action`: `v2` → `v3` (build-daily, build-release, docker-manual)
- `docker/setup-buildx-action`: `v2` → `v3` (build-daily, build-release, docker-manual)
- `docker/login-action`: `v2` → `v3` (build-daily, build-release, docker-manual)
- `docker/build-push-action`: `v4` → `v6` (build-release, docker-manual)
- `getsentry/action-release`: `v1.2.1` → `v3` (build-daily, build-release)
- `revam/gh-action-get-tag-and-version`: `v1` → `v3` (tray-standalone-manual)

**Breaking input fix (revam v1 → v3):**
`revam/gh-action-get-tag-and-version@v3` renamed all inputs; unrecognised
names are silently ignored, which would have produced a blank version string:
- `tag:` → `use_tag_ref:` — the git ref/tag to resolve
- `prefixRegex:` → `prefix_regex:` — pattern used to strip the leading `v`

`build-daily.yml` and `build-release.yml` already used v3 input names
for this action and were not affected.

`chore` (`abstractions`): **Bump abstractions version to 6.0.0-alpha.46**.

`fix` (`db`): **Make queue migrations run on SQL Server and MySQL**:

EF Core filters migrations by exact DbContext type via [DbContext] attribute.
All migration Designer files are tagged with [DbContext(typeof(SqliteQueueDbContext))],
so SqlServerQueueDbContext and MySqlQueueDbContext found zero applicable migrations —
MigrateAsync silently no-oped, Jobs table was never created, and WorkerPoolManager
crashed on LoadAllAsync.

Secondary issue: migration files hard-coded SQLite affinity type strings (TEXT, INTEGER).
SQL Server rejects TEXT as a PRIMARY KEY type, so even if migrations were discovered they
would have failed at CREATE TABLE.

- Add QueueMigrationsAssembly: extends MigrationsAssembly, overrides Migrations to
  find SqliteQueueDbContext-tagged migrations regardless of the current provider context
- Register via ReplaceService in SqlServerQueueDbContext and MySqlQueueDbContext
- Remove explicit type strings from InitialCreate and AddJobChainContext migrations;
  EF Core now infers provider-appropriate DDL (uniqueidentifier/nvarchar/bigint on SQL
  Server, char(36)/varchar/bigint on MySQL, TEXT/INTEGER on SQLite unchanged)

`refactor` (`db`): **Implement `IMigrationsAssembly` directly in `QueueMigrationsAssembly`**:

Replaced the `MigrationsAssembly` subclass (EF Core internal API, `EF1001`) with a
direct `IMigrationsAssembly` implementation. This removes the dependency on an unstable
internal constructor and makes the migration-discovery re-routing fully public-API-only.

- Dropped inheritance from `MigrationsAssembly` and the `EF1001` pragma
- Removed the DI constructor; EF Core resolves the class via its default constructor
- Added `ModelSnapshot` (discovers `SqliteQueueDbContextModelSnapshot` by `[DbContext]` attribute)
- Added `FindMigrationId` (exact-match or suffix-match, mirrors EF Core convention)
- Added `CreateMigration` (sets `ActiveProvider` as EF Core`s own implementation does)
- Switched `LazyInitializer` to `Lazy<T>` for cleaner nullability on `ModelSnapshot`

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Set DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType, DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName, DOTNET_EnableCrashReport,
and DOTNET_CrashReportPath in  so crash dumps land in
. Also add  before the server loop.
Container still needs  at runtime for .NET to
write the dump.

`fix`: **Use raw version as tag if no tag field**.

`chore` (`abstractions`): **Bump abstractions version to 6.0.0-alpha.45**.
`misc`: **For some reason, UIX_VideoLocal_Hash index was existing on my database, so check it in the migrations**.
`misc`: **Convert system timers to recurring jobs**.

`feat` (`api`): **Replace github api with manifest-based updates**:

Replaced the GitHub API (releases, tags, commits) with manifest
JSON files served from the `metadata` branch on both the WebUI
and server repos. This removes the direct dependency on the GitHub
API and the GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable.

`repo` (`manifest`): **Add manifest update workflows and script**:

Add update-manifest.mjs to manage the server manifest on the metadata
branch, and add update-manifest jobs to both build-daily.yml and
build-release.yml that run before sentry-upload. Daily builds prepend
a Dev entry and rotate to at most 30 entries, stable releases use the
release body as release notes, and commit messages use the repo(manifest)
format.

`repo` (`workflows`): **Fix dependency graph in build flow**.
`misc`: *'Use Case-Sensitive Collation for Places Where Needed.**
